title: 1:1 Seren Voss + Piper Bergmann — 2026-11-30
compiled_truth:
Weekly 1:1 between [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) (CTO) and [Piper Bergmann](people/piper-bergmann) (Head of Developer Relations) at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor). Meeting held over video — Seren was working remotely from the Portland office while Piper was at HQ.

The first half of the conversation centered on the developer preview program that Piper's team launched earlier in November. Adoption numbers are tracking above expectations: 214 signups from independent developers and 38 from teams at mid-market companies. Piper flagged that feedback on the onboarding docs has been mixed — several developers reported confusion around the single-pass primitives integration path, specifically how to handle edge cases in partial payload scenarios. Seren agreed this is a priority and committed to pairing with the docs team the following week to rewrite the integration guide's troubleshooting section.

They spent some time discussing the upcoming developer conference appearance in January. Piper proposed a live demo format rather than a slide-heavy talk, arguing it would resonate better with the infrastructure crowd. Seren was initially hesitant — live demos carry risk — but came around after Piper suggested a fallback recorded version they could cut to if anything broke. They agreed to do a dry run in mid-December.

The second half shifted to team dynamics. Piper mentioned she's been stretched thin since the DevRel team lost a contractor in October, and the content pipeline is suffering. She asked whether there's budget to bring on a technical writer, even part-time. Seren said she'd raise it with the ops team but couldn't promise anything before Q1 planning wraps. In the meantime, Seren suggested pulling in one of the junior engineers for a two-week rotation to help with code samples and tutorial content — something they'd experimented with before.

Piper also brought up a concern about community sentiment on the developer forum. A few vocal users have been pushing for an open-source release of the core codec libraries. Seren's position remains firm: the core stays proprietary for now, but they could explore open-sourcing some of the peripheral tooling (CLI utilities, SDK wrappers). Piper will draft a community response post for Seren to reveiw before it goes live.

Meeting ended with a quick check-in on Piper's workload and general morale. She described it as "manageable but barely" — Seren acknowledged this and reiterated she'd push for headcount.
timeline: - **2026-10-15** | DevRel contractor departure leaves [Piper Bergmann](people/piper-bergmann)'s team short-staffed
- **2026-11-04** | Developer preview program officially launched by DevRel at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor)
- **2026-11-18** | Piper reports 150+ signups in first two weeks of preview program
- **2026-11-30** | 1:1 between [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) and Piper; discussion on docs, conference prep, and headcount
- **2026-12-08** | Seren joins docs team for integration guide rewrite sprint
- **2026-12-16** | Dry run of live demo for January conference talk
- **2027-01-09** | Community response post on open-source question published on Anchor developer forum
- **2027-01-22** | Piper and Seren deliver live demo at developer infrastructure conference
- **2027-02-03** | Q1 planning concludes; part-time technical writer role approved for DevRel team
